templates.name: Reference · Data single (chart-led)

templates.name: Reference · Infographic: 3-step flow
templates.description: REFERENCE LAYOUT — three variant compositions of a STEP-BY-STEP infographic. A: numbered step label + detail panel rows. B: centered vertical-spine timeline with alternating cards. C: 3 steps + a tier

templates.name: Reference · Infographic: Annotated mock
templates.description: REFERENCE LAYOUT — three variant compositions of an ANNOTATED-DIAGRAM infographic: a central stylised UI mock surrounded by callout labels. A: central mock with callouts left & right. B: mock + number

templates.name: Reference · Infographic: Framework roadmap
templates.description: REFERENCE LAYOUT — three variant compositions of a ROADMAP/FRAMEWORK-MAP infographic. A: horizontal milestone roadmap (numbered phases on a timeline). B: linear flow of connected nodes left-to-right,

templates.name: Reference · Infographic: Matrix / table
templates.description: REFERENCE LAYOUT — three variant compositions of a MATRIX/TABLE infographic. A: 2×2 quadrant matrix on two labelled axes (e.g. effort vs impact). B: comparison table (rows × columns) with one column e

templates.name: Reference · Infographic: Tree (hierarchy)
templates.description: REFERENCE LAYOUT — three variant compositions of a HIERARCHY infographic: a root idea branching into 3 categories, each with leaf items. A: full case-study tree (root → 3 columns of leaf cards). B: ai

templates.name: Reference · Layered / stack visual
templates.description: REFERENCE LAYOUT — three variant compositions of a single visual built on a LAYERED/STACK metaphor. A: funnel (narrowing stages = drop-off). B: pyramid (vision → strategy → tactics, top-down). C: stac

templates.name: Reference · Pictograph (count metaphor)
templates.description: REFERENCE LAYOUT — three variant compositions of a single visual built on a QUANTITY metaphor. A: one unit vs a crowd. B: share-of-total (a few filled icons in a muted grid + ratio). C: ratio row + gi

templates.name: Reference · Testimonial (proof)

templates.name: Reference · Trajectory (path metaphor)
